metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Resolve the foundational-first vs. integrated-suite-ownership contradiction in 81b4: either reassign the 'full test suite passes with all five siblings integrated' AC to a separate integration milestone / the last-landing child, or reword the AC to clarify it is verified as a post-integration step after all siblings merge.
findings.finding: Child 81b4-985e-b3e8-4026 is designated as the foundational story that must land FIRST, yet its own AC includes: 'The full test suite passes with all five sibling changes integrated (this foundational story owns the integrated-suite check).' This AC cannot be verified at the time 81b4 lands — it requires all five sibling stories to already be merged. The foundational-first requirement and the integrated-suite-ownership requirement are directly contradictory as written.
findings.suggested_fix: Move the 'full test suite passes with all five sibling changes integrated' AC out of 81b4 and into a post-merge integration checklist item on the parent epic, or assign it to the last child in the landing order (e.g., 2918-24c8-4c2a-4e10, which already depends on both 81b4 and eced and has the latest dependency chain).

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] For each of the five child-story ACs (items 1–5), add at least one concrete, in-plan observable (e.g., a specific test name/file, a CLI command and expected output, or a named code symbol) so the parent plan is self-sufficient for completion verification.
findings.finding: AC items 1–5 ('Child story X is closed and certified') delegate all measurability to child-story definitions that are not present in this plan. An agent or reviewer working from this document alone cannot determine what observable artifact or deterministic check constitutes 'certified' for each child story, making the parent plan's completion criteria non-self-evidently verifiable.
findings.suggested_fix: Augment each child-story AC bullet with a brief parenthetical stating the primary in-repo artifact that proves it done — e.g., '…is closed and certified (evidenced by `IDEA_STATUS` constant in `types.ts`, exclusion test in `dispatch.test.ts` passing, and `make test` green)'.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Assign the CLAUDE.md documentation update to one of the five child stories (or add a sixth) so every AC criterion has a corresponding decomposition work item.
findings.finding: The AC requires 'The committed `CLAUDE.md` documents the `idea` status and its lifecycle,' but none of the five decomposed child stories names CLAUDE.md documentation as a deliverable, leaving this criterion with no assigned work in the decomposition.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — `CLAUDE.md` bullet; Decomposition — five child stories
findings.suggested_fix: Extend child story 1 ('status vocabulary + dispatch exclusion') or the atomic `idea` command story to explicitly include 'update CLAUDE.md with the `idea` status definition and lifecycle notes' as a deliverable.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Define the 'documented workflow-transition prerequisite' (e.g., Jira admin must create an `IDEA` status in the project workflow) in the Context or child story 4, and add an AC item that verifies it is met or documented.
findings.finding: The Success Criteria references a 'documented workflow-transition prerequisite' for the Jira round-trip, but this prerequisite is never defined in the Context, not assigned to any child story, and not verified by the corresponding AC item ('Jira `idea ↔ IDEA` mapping is closed and certified'), leaving an unnamed external dependency untracked.
findings.location: Success Criteria — Jira bullet; Decomposition — child story 4; Context

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Clarify in child story 5 whether the atomic `idea` command is intentionally epic-only (and document this as a deliberate scope choice) or should accept an optional `--type` argument to honor the 'any ticket type' design principle.
findings.finding: Context states 'any ticket type can hold [the `idea` status]' (it is a status, not a type), but the atomic `idea` command decomposition specifies 'single genesis CREATE, type `epic`' — creating a tension about whether the command accepts non-epic ticket types, which may cause implementers to hard-code `epic` and silently violate the broader design intent.
findings.location: Context — 'It is a *status* (any ticket type can hold it)'; Decomposition — child story 5 '(single genesis CREATE, type `epic`)'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an explicit rollback/back-out plan for the Jira `idea ↔ IDEA` mapping: specify what to do if the Jira `IDEA` workflow status is absent at deploy time (e.g., graceful degradation, feature-gate the mapping, or an expand-contract sequence where the Jira side is set up first).
findings.finding: The Jira `idea ↔ IDEA` mapping child story explicitly depends on a 'workflow-transition prerequisite' (the `IDEA` status must exist in the Jira project's workflow), but the plan provides no rollback/back-out plan for the scenario where the code-side status maps are merged but the Jira-side workflow has not been configured. Per T4, acknowledging a breakage risk is insufficient — a reversibility mechanism must be specified.
findings.location: Child story: 'Jira `idea ↔ IDEA` mapping'; Success Criteria section; 'subject to the documented workflow-transition prerequisite'
findings.suggested_fix: Sequence the child story as expand-then-contract: (1) add the Jira `IDEA` workflow status as a prerequisite step with a verification check in CI/smoke tests, (2) only then merge the rebar status-map changes. Document the rollback as: revert the status-map code change and reclassify any `idea` tickets to `open` via a one-time migration script.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an explicit documentation deliverable (e.g., a section in README, CLAUDE.md, or a dedicated runbook) that specifies the Jira administrator setup steps required before `idea ↔ IDEA` sync works, and reference it from the Jira mapping child story's AC.
findings.finding: The plan references 'the documented workflow-transition prerequisite' for the Jira `IDEA` status as if this documentation already exists or will exist, but never specifies where it is or will be written. The CLAUDE.md AC covers status lifecycle for developers/agents but does not address the operator/admin setup instructions needed to configure the Jira workflow.
findings.location: Success Criteria: 'idea round-trips to Jira IDEA (subject to the documented workflow-transition prerequisite)'; Acceptance Criteria: 'The committed CLAUDE.md documents the idea status and its lifecycle'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add at least one end-to-end scenario AC that validates the team-facing workflow (e.g., 'given a mix of `open` and `idea` tickets, `ready` and `next-batch` return zero `idea` tickets, `validate` reports zero idea-attributable findings, and `list` returns all idea tickets') to provide a concrete before/after validation mechanism.
findings.finding: No concrete before/after validation mechanism is specified for the user-facing workflow improvement: all ACs are child-story closure checkboxes and lint/test pass, with no scenario (e.g., 'given N idea tickets in backlog, `validate` reports zero additional findings and `ready` returns empty for those tickets') that demonstrates the feature working end-to-end from a team's perspective.
findings.suggested_fix: Add an AC such as: '[ ] An end-to-end integration test (or documented manual dogfood scenario) confirms: (1) creating an `idea` ticket via the atomic command never makes it appear in `ready`/`next-batch`, (2) `validate` on a project with only `idea`-status epics/orphans reports no idea-attributable findings, and (3) `idea → closed` succeeds without completion gates.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an explicit sequencing note (or ordering constraint in the AC) establishing that Story 1 ('status vocabulary + dispatch exclusion') must be merged before Stories 2, 3, and 5 are integrated, to preserve the 'never momentarily claimable' guarantee.
findings.finding: The AC lists all five child stories as unordered peers, but Stories 2, 3, and 5 each depend on Story 1 (status vocabulary + dispatch exclusion) being merged first to preserve the plan's own guarantee that an `idea` ticket is 'never momentarily claimable'. If Story 5 (atomic `idea` command) is deployed before Story 1's dispatch exclusion is live, the genesis CREATE would produce a ticket that is immediately eligible for dispatch, directly violating the stated Success Criterion.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — all five child-story AC lines; Success Criteria — 'never momentarily claimable'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify that the `idea → closed` gate bypass keys off the ticket's **current status** (`current_status == 'idea'`) at each of the four gate sites, not off `ticket_type`, since any ticket type can hold `idea` status — and identify each gate site by name (completion-verifier in `_completion_precheck`, signature gate in `_signature_gate`/`transition_core`, plan-review in `gates.plan_review_precheck`, bug-close-reason in `transition_core`).
findings.finding: The plan's `idea → closed` gate-bypass mechanism is under-specified: existing completion/attestation exemptions are TYPE-keyed (`session_log`, `bug`), but `idea` is a STATUS that any ticket type can hold. The four gates that need bypassing (`_completion_precheck`, `_signature_gate`, `bug_close_reason`, plan-review precheck) each inspect `ticket_type`, not the ticket's current status. Without explicitly specifying that the bypass must key off `current_status == 'idea'` (read before the lock in `transition_core` / before `_completion_precheck`), an implementer following the existing exemption pattern will either add a spurious new type check or miss one of the gate sites.
findings.location: Plan section: '`idea → closed` gate bypass (the four completion/attestation close gates)'; code: src/rebar/_commands/transition_close.py `_completion_precheck`, src/rebar/_commands/txn.py `transition_core` / `_signature_gate`, src/rebar/_commands/gates.py `plan_review_precheck`
findings.suggested_fix: In each gate-bypass check, add an `or current_status == 'idea'` branch (or pass a pre-read `is_idea_close: bool` flag through the call chain). For `transition_close.close_ticket`, read the current status from the reducer before the lock and pass it into `_completion_precheck`. For `transition_core`, add the same status read before the bug-close and signature gate calls.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Explicitly name `inbound_differ._JIRA_TO_LOCAL_STATUS` as one of the five maps requiring `'IDEA': 'idea'`, and confirm the existing parity test in `tests/unit/rebar_reconciler/test_config.py` will be updated to assert the `IDEA ↔ idea` entry is present in both `config.jira_to_local_status` and `inbound_differ._JIRA_TO_LOCAL_STATUS`.
findings.finding: The plan's Jira child story references 'five status maps' but does not acknowledge the lock-step constraint between `config.jira_to_local_status` and `inbound_differ._JIRA_TO_LOCAL_STATUS` — two independently-maintained maps that the codebase itself comments 'Must stay in lock-step'. If an implementer updates `config.py` but misses `inbound_differ.py` (or vice versa), inbound reconciliation will silently map `IDEA` Jira issues to `open` (the fallback default in `_jira_status_to_local`).
findings.location: Plan section: 'Jira `idea ↔ IDEA` mapping (five status maps + parity test)'; code: src/rebar/_engine/rebar_reconciler/config.py line 45, src/rebar/_engine/rebar_reconciler/inbound_differ.py lines 128-137

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify and implement the reconciler's behavior when the Jira project lacks the `IDEA` workflow status — either fail-closed with a diagnostic, skip inbound status mutation for unknown idea-mapped statuses, or document the exact corruption risk and add a `bridge-fsck` check for it.
findings.finding: The plan asserts 'idea round-trips to Jira IDEA (subject to the documented workflow-transition prerequisite)' but leaves unverified what the inbound reconciler does when the Jira project lacks an IDEA workflow status: the existing `inbound_translate._jira_status_to_local` and `inbound_differ._JIRA_TO_LOCAL_STATUS` both fall back silently to 'open' for any unknown Jira status name, meaning a reconciler pass against a Jira project without the IDEA workflow status would corrupt idea tickets (flipping them to 'open', making them claimable) with no error signal.
findings.location: Plan section: 'Jira `idea ↔ IDEA` mapping (five status maps + parity test)' / Success Criteria: '`idea` round-trips to Jira `IDEA` (subject to the documented workflow-transition prerequisite)'
findings.suggested_fix: Add `'IDEA': 'idea'` to both `config.jira_to_local_status` and `inbound_differ._JIRA_TO_LOCAL_STATUS`, and add a preflight check in `reconcile.py` (analogous to the existing `local_to_jira_status` preflight scan) that warns or skips inbound status updates for the `idea` local status when no corresponding Jira workflow status is found.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify in the 'atomic `idea` command' child story exactly how the `idea` status is set atomically at creation (e.g., extend CREATE event schema to carry `initial_status`, or write a fused CREATE+STATUS under the same git commit lock), and verify this does not introduce a transient 'open' state during reducer replay.
findings.finding: The 'atomic `idea` command' child story specifies 'single genesis CREATE, type `epic`' as its implementation, but the existing CREATE event processor (`process_create`) never sets `status` — initial state is always `status='open'` from `make_initial_state()`. No prior art exists in the codebase for a CREATE event that yields a non-'open' initial status. The plan does not specify whether (a) the CREATE event schema is extended to carry `initial_status`, (b) a paired STATUS event is written atomically with the CREATE, or (c) some other mechanism is used — leaving a load-bearing implementation detail unresolved.
findings.location: Plan section: 'atomic `idea` command across CLI/MCP/library (single genesis CREATE, type `epic`)'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify, in the 'Jira `idea ↔ IDEA` mapping' child story, the exact deploy prerequisite (e.g., 'Jira project admin must add `IDEA` status to the project workflow BEFORE this story is merged') and either add a graceful fallback (e.g., skip/warn rather than abort when the `IDEA` transition is unavailable) or define a sequenced rollout order so the external Jira-side change lands first.
findings.finding: The plan states `idea` round-trips to Jira `IDEA` 'subject to the documented workflow-transition prerequisite' but does not specify where that prerequisite is documented, how an implementer or operator verifies it is satisfied before deploying the status map, or what happens at runtime when the Jira project workflow lacks an `IDEA` status. The existing `transition_issue_by_name` raises a hard `RuntimeError` and the out-of-scope reconciler bug causes a mid-pass abort — meaning a missing Jira-side `IDEA` workflow status will cause every reconciler pass touching an `idea` ticket to abort. No graceful fallback, sequenced deploy order, or operator verification step is specified.
findings.location: Success Criteria: 'idea round-trips to Jira IDEA (subject to the documented workflow-transition prerequisite)'; Child story: 'Jira idea ↔ IDEA mapping (five status maps + parity test)'
findings.suggested_fix: Add to the child story's AC: 'The Jira project workflow has `IDEA` configured (admin step, verified via `rebar bridge-probe`) before the `idea: IDEA` entry is added to `local_to_jira_status`.' Alternatively, have `transition_issue_by_name` log-and-skip (rather than raise) when a transition to `IDEA` is unavailable, treating the Jira sync as best-effort for the new status until the workflow is ready.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Child story 1 ('status vocabulary') must explicitly list `src/rebar/schemas/common.schema.json` (and the four `$ref`-ing schemas) as file-impact targets and add `"idea"` to the `ticket_status` enum in each.
findings.finding: The `idea` status value is referenced throughout the plan as a first-class status (dispatch exclusion, transition validator, list filter, Jira mapping, CLI command), but the authoritative `ticket_status` JSON Schema enum in `src/rebar/schemas/common.schema.json` currently enumerates exactly six values (`["open", "in_progress", "blocked", "closed", "archived", "deleted"]`) and `"idea"` is absent. Child story 1 is labelled "status vocabulary" but the plan never names `common.schema.json` (or the other locations where this enum is echoed, such as `transition_result.schema.json`, `ticket_state.schema.json`, `ticket_state_llm.schema.json`, `claim_result.schema.json`) as explicit file-impact targets. An implementer following only the plan text could add `idea` to Python logic while leaving the JSON Schema enum unchanged, causing schema-validation failures at every tool boundary that uses the validator.
findings.location: Context section / Child story 'status vocabulary + dispatch exclusion' / src/rebar/schemas/common.schema.json line 9
findings.suggested_fix: In `src/rebar/schemas/common.schema.json` line 9, change the enum to `["open", "in_progress", "blocked", "closed", "archived", "deleted", "idea"]`. Apply the same change to any schema file that inlines the status enum rather than `$ref`-ing it.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Child story 4 ('Jira `idea ↔ IDEA` mapping') must explicitly enumerate all five map locations by file+variable name and state the intended behaviour when the Jira `IDEA` workflow status is absent (e.g., whether `idea` tickets are added to `excluded_statuses` as a fallback or whether a per-ticket skip is used instead of aborting the full reconciler pass via `preflight_status_mapping`).
findings.finding: The plan's Jira child story references "five status maps" without naming them, but the codebase contains at least five distinct places where status values are hard-coded: (1) `config.local_to_jira_status`, (2) `config.jira_to_local_status`, (3) `inbound_differ._JIRA_TO_LOCAL_STATUS`, (4) `inbound_differ._REBAR_STATUS_LABEL_TO_LOCAL`, and (5) the `excluded_statuses` default in `outbound_differ.compute_outbound_mutations` (`{"archived", "deleted"}`). These maps have materially different semantics: maps 1–3 translate between local and Jira status strings, map 4 handles annotation labels, and map 5 controls whether a ticket is synced to Jira at all. The plan's phrase 'five status maps + parity test' does not specify whether `idea` tickets should appear in `excluded_statuses` (skipping Jira sync entirely) or be mapped to a Jira `IDEA` workflow status via maps 1–3 — these are mutually exclusive designs with different behaviour if the `IDEA` Jira workflow status is absent.
findings.location: Context section: 'Jira `idea ↔ IDEA` mapping (five status maps + parity test)' / src/rebar/_engine/rebar_reconciler/config.py / src/rebar/_engine/rebar_reconciler/inbound_differ.py / src/rebar/_engine/rebar_reconciler/outbound_differ.py

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Child story 2 ('`idea → closed` gate bypass') must explicitly enumerate all gate checks by name (including the file-impact/referencing-commit precheck in `transition_close.py`) and state the intended behaviour for each — specifying whether each is bypassed or retained for `idea → closed`.
findings.finding: The plan says `idea → closed` should bypass the four completion/attestation close gates, but names them only generically ('the four completion/attestation close gates'). The codebase shows the close path involves at least: (1) the completion-verification gate (`require_completion_verification_for_close`), (2) the signature gate (`require_signature_for_close`), (3) the bug-close-reason guard, and (4) the file-impact/referencing-commit precheck. No fallback or error behavior is specified for what happens when the `idea → closed` bypass is attempted but the ticket has unexpected state (e.g., an `idea` ticket that acquired a `file_impact` field). The plan's AC only says 'the structural open-children guard still holds' with no guidance on the file-impact precheck which is structurally similar but semantically distinct.
findings.location: Context section: '`idea → closed` gate bypass (the four completion/attestation close gates; keep the structural open-children guard)' / src/rebar/_commands/transition_close.py lines 78–196

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an explicit `depends_on` link to 81b4-985e-b3e8-4026 in each of 1982-bf75-0f28-4e91, eced-c6b8-7461-48a0, and 6173-32ea-1fc0-4f04, mirroring the pattern already present in 2918-24c8-4c2a-4e10.
findings.finding: Children 1982-bf75-0f28-4e91, eced-c6b8-7461-48a0, and 6173-32ea-1fc0-4f04 each implicitly require 81b4-985e-b3e8-4026 to have landed first (all three consume the `idea` status value that 81b4 establishes in the type system), but none of the three explicitly records a depends_on link to 81b4. Only 2918-24c8-4c2a-4e10 states this prerequisite explicitly.
findings.suggested_fix: In each of 1982, eced, and 6173 add a 'Prerequisite (depends on S1, 81b4-985e-b3e8-4026)' section identical in structure to the one in 2918, and record the depends_on link in the ticket metadata so the dispatch surface respects the ordering.

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)
